title: AI identifies interactions in CRISPR complexes to improve specificity of DNA editing
excerpt: AlphaFold3 predictions of the molecular contacts made by a genome-editing enzyme have been used to improve the enzyme’s selectivity for target DNA sequences.
content_html:
• NEWS AND VIEWS
  • 22 July 2026

AlphaFold3 predictions of the molecular contacts made by a genome-editing enzyme have been used to improve the enzyme’s selectivity for target DNA sequences.

By

  1. Hoi Yee Chu

    1. Hoi Yee Chu is in the School of Biomedical Sciences, The University of Hong Kong, and the Centre for Oncology and Immunology, Hong Kong Science Park, Hong Kong, China.

  2. Alan S. L. Wong

    1. Alan S. L. Wong is in the School of Biomedical Sciences, The University of Hong Kong, and the Centre for Oncology and Immunology, Hong Kong Science Park, Hong Kong, China.

Tiny shifts of less than one ten-billionth of a metre in the gap between an enzyme and its target might seem too subtle to measure directly, or to predict with artificial intelligence. Yet such shifts are exactly what Meng et al.1, writing in Nature, have leveraged to improve the fidelity of ‘base editors’ — a class of genome-rewriting tools that can make single-nucleotide changes at targeted sites, offering potential for treating many genetic diseases. By using AI to predict the structures of thousands of base editors in complex with nucleic acids, and analysing the patterns that emerged, the authors have captured the differences in molecular interactions that correlate with on-target versus off-target editing.

AlphaFold3 predictions of the molecular contacts made by a genome-editing enzyme have been used to improve the enzyme’s selectivity for target DNA sequences.

sentences.en: AlphaFold3 predictions of the molecular contacts made by a genome-editing enzyme have been used to improve the enzyme’s selectivity for target DNA sequences.
sentences.zh: AlphaFold3 对基因组编辑酶产生的分子接触的预测已被用来提高酶对目标 DNA 序列的选择性。

blocks.html_en: Tiny shifts of less than one ten-billionth of a metre in the gap between an enzyme and its target might seem too subtle to measure directly, or to predict with artificial intelligence. Yet such shifts are exactly what Meng et al.1, writing in Nature, have leveraged to improve the fidelity of ‘base editors’ — a class of genome-rewriting tools that can make single-nucleotide changes at targeted sites, offering potential for treating many genetic diseases. By using AI to predict the structures of thousands of base editors in complex with nucleic acids, and analysing the patterns that emerged, the authors have captured the differences in molecular interactions that correlate with on-target versus off-target editing.

sentences.en: Tiny shifts of less than one ten-billionth of a metre in the gap between an enzyme and its target might seem too subtle to measure directly, or to predict with artificial intelligence.
sentences.zh: 酶与其目标之间的间隙小于十亿分之一米的微小变化可能看起来太微妙，无法直接测量或用人工智能进行预测。

sentences.en: Yet such shifts are exactly what Meng et al.1, writing in Nature, have leveraged to improve the fidelity of ‘base editors’ — a class of genome-rewriting tools that can make single-nucleotide changes at targeted sites, offering potential for treating many genetic diseases.
sentences.zh: 然而，这种转变正是孟等人在《自然》杂志上发表的文章所利用的，以提高“碱基编辑器”的保真度。“碱基编辑器”是一类基因组重写工具，可以在目标位点进行单核苷酸变化，为治疗许多遗传疾病提供了潜力。

sentences.en: By using AI to predict the structures of thousands of base editors in complex with nucleic acids, and analysing the patterns that emerged, the authors have captured the differences in molecular interactions that correlate with on-target versus off-target editing.
sentences.zh: 通过使用人工智能预测与核酸复合的数千个碱基编辑器的结构，并分析出现的模式，作者捕获了与靶向编辑和脱靶编辑相关的分子相互作用的差异。

blocks.html_en: Editorial 22 JUL 26

content_html_zh:
• 新闻和观点
  • 22 July 2026

AlphaFold3 对基因组编辑酶产生的分子接触的预测已被用来提高酶对目标 DNA 序列的选择性。

By

  1. 海怡珠 (Hoi Yee Chu) 海怡珠在香港大学生物医学学院以及中国香港科学园肿瘤学和免疫学中心工作。
  2. 艾伦·S。 L。 黄艾伦 S. L。 黄博士在香港大学生物医学学院以及中国香港科学园肿瘤学和免疫学中心工作。

酶与其目标之间的间隙小于十亿分之一米的微小变化可能看起来太微妙，无法直接测量或用人工智能进行预测。 然而，这种转变正是孟等人在《自然》杂志上发表的文章所利用的，以提高“碱基编辑器”的保真度。“碱基编辑器”是一类基因组重写工具，可以在目标位点进行单核苷酸变化，为治疗许多遗传疾病提供了潜力。 通过使用人工智能预测与核酸复合的数千个碱基编辑器的结构，并分析出现的模式，作者捕获了与靶向编辑和脱靶编辑相关的分子相互作用的差异。
